route: 3 - ANISOTROPIC COUNT-RECURSION (birth-level telescoping in the spectral/elliptic metric)
target: tight linear law c_k(q,rho) <= C*rho + 1, level-uniform, small C (load-bearing c_k(4.44)<=12)
headline: The isotropic recursion diverged because it bounded THIS-level refill by g=4 charges per nearby anchor: coefficient (4/9)(1+g)=20/9>1. The EXACT birth-level telescoping dissolves the (1+g): refill at level k is exactly b_k (one level's stitch), and the 'nearby anchors' ARE previous levels' refill, already partitioned. No charge multiplication => coefficient is E*S with a SINGLE slope E and a CONVERGENT geometric sum S. Anisotropy enters as the SPECTRAL contraction 1/3 (all eigenvalues of M have modulus 3), giving S=Sum||M^-j||inf=1.677 < isotropic 9/5.

exact_algebra.eigenvalues: 3 (x-block) and roots of lam^2+lam+9 (disc -35) both of modulus sqrt(9)=3 => ALL eigenvalues modulus exactly 3 (elliptic 2x2 block = 3 x irrational rotation arccos(-1/6))
exact_algebra.kappa_bounded: sup_j ||(3 M^-1)^j||inf = 1.549 < inf  => elliptic, no polynomial growth (rigorous no-blow-up of the pull-back norms)

telescoping_identity_EXACT.statement: c_k(q,rho) = sum_{j>=0} #{Stitch_{k-j} intersect M^-j B(q,rho)} = sum_j t_j(q,rho), t_j := #{birth-(k-j) walk points in B(q,rho)}
telescoping_identity_EXACT.why_exact: Walk_k = M.Walk_{k-1} DISJOINT-UNION Stitch_k (proven); M rescales, preserving birth level. Every walk point has a unique birth level => the birth-decomposition is a PARTITION. Verified anchors_k == M(walk_{k-1}) elementwise at L6,L7,L8.
telescoping_identity_EXACT.measured_termwise_bound: t_j(rho) <= E*rho*||M^-j||inf with E=3 holds at L6,L7 exactly; L8 has 2 off-by-one lattice-granularity cases (t_2=1 vs 0.44; t_2=2 vs 1.78) => the honest bound is t_j <= E*rho*||M^-j||inf + 1, with only ~log_3(rho) nonempty pull-back levels.

closing_coefficient.formula: C* = E * S,  E = single-level refill slope (b_k(r) <= E*r, ONE level's stitch on 3-separated anchors)
closing_coefficient.vs_isotropic: isotropic gave (4/9)(1+g)=20/9>1 (DIVERGES) or, via Model-B menu-constant, C*=(9/5)E=5.40. Anisotropic: C*=S*E=1.677*E, no g, convergent.
closing_coefficient.thin_region_refinement: the pulled-back regions M^-j(cube) are THIN sheared parallelepipeds; 1-D walk-arcs pierce them less than an inf-ball of equal inf-radius. Measured birth-decay S'_meas = max c/t0 = 1.50-1.56 < S=1.677. With load-bearing refill slope E_mid[rho in 4,5]~2: C_tight ~ E_mid*S'_meas = 2.70/3.50/3.38 (L6/L7/L8).
closing_coefficient.constant_delivered: C = E*S. Rigorous (E=3): 5.03. Tight/measured (E~2, S'~1.5): ~2.7-3.0 == measured tight constant.

anisotropic_overcount_TEST.what: isotropic anchor bound = #{parent in inf-ball B(M^-1 q,(4/9)rho)} vs TRUE anchor count a_k(q,rho)=#{anchors in B(q,rho)} (=exact parallelepiped pull-back)
anisotropic_overcount_TEST.reading: the isotropic 4/9 inf-enclosure over-counts the true anisotropic pull-back count by up to ~43% at large rho, level-stable; direct evidence the parallelepiped is genuinely thinner than its enclosing inf-ball.

key_lemma: SINGLE-LEVEL refill slope: b_k(r) <= E*r for every level k (E~2-3), where b_k counts ONE level's stitch interiors (short connector-word arcs) placed on the 3-separated anchor set A_k. NO induction hypothesis, NO cross-level g-charge, NO circular anchor count. For the TIGHT constant, additionally the thin-parallelepiped decay S'_meas<S (1-D arcs pierce M^-j(cube) less than its enclosing inf-ball).
gap: The telescoping + spectral S is a rigorous reduction that beats isotropic (5.03<5.40) and DISSOLVES the g-charge divergence, but does not by itself reach the sharp C~2.5: (i) it uses the worst-case E=3 (rho=1 slope); the load-bearing E~2 is measured not proven; (ii) the thin-region saving S'_meas~1.5<S=1.677 is measured not proven (it is the same 1-D arc-incidence / anti-stacking fact that no route has promoted to a theorem). Reduces the ENTIRE tight law to the single-level refill slope + thin-region decay, both level-stable across L6/L7/L8.
scripts: design/tight/aniso_measure.py  (birth-tagged walks L5-L8, c_k/a_k/b_k, birth-decomp t_j, isotropic-vs-anisotropic anchor overcount; PYTHONPATH=. pypy3 aniso_measure.py 6 7 8)
design/tight/aniso_summary.py  (exact S via fractions, per-level E/S'/C_iso/C_aniso/C_meas, termwise-bound check; python3 aniso_summary.py)
